mentions.note: Trades at just 12x this year's earnings estimates with a continuing turnaround; Cramer wants the multi-year move, not the one-day pop.
Benefiting from deregulation and multiple expansion after two decades of financial-sector punishment.
Gone from ugly duckling to beautiful swan; analysts haven't caught up, so upside estimate revisions keep driving the stock higher.
35% EPS growth continues Jane Fraser's turnaround; still far cheaper than peers even after last year's 66% rally — Cramer's top pick of the group.
Loved by Wall Street; estimates always too low; stock jumps predictably like 'trained rabbit'; likely to jump higher next week on earnings.
Bank sector absurdly undervalued: Citigroup +52% earnings growth at 12.4× earnings vs. S&P 500 22× with 17% growth. Pro-bank admin set to unlock M&A wave; huge valuation gap.
Real bank showed resilience alongside JPM and PNC; strong bank leadership signals economic resilience and consumer financial stability.
CEO Jane Frasier has rationalized bank and made it perennial top performer; new intraday high shows strength despite red close.
